summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 7 warning, 12 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:12-78` — Custom YAML parser is a long-term maintenance risk if the runbook schema grows beyond flat key/list-of-objects. Document the supported subset so contributors know when to switch to a real parser.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:247` — `matchRunbook` tie-break is a dense compound conditional. Extract a named comparator for auditability.
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:815-825` — Console interception pattern used in 4 places. `node:test`'s `mock.method(console, 'warn')` would auto-restore and be more idiomatic.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:110` — `isSafeRegex` heuristic misses alternation-based ReDoS (e.g. `(a|a)+$`). Acceptable for v1 with trusted YAML; track for hardening.

🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:962` — No test verifies built-in runbook patterns match realistic descriptions (e.g., "add a new CLI command" triggers `add-cli-command`). Structure is tested, but shipped pattern scoring is not. Carried forward from task-10-tester — recommend filing as future work.

🟡 test/runbooks.test.mjs:960 — No smoke test for built-in patterns against realistic descriptions (e.g., `matchRunbook("add a new CLI command", builtIns)` should return `add-cli-command`). Carried forward from task-10-tester; a pattern regression would go undetected.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:110` — Pre-existing: `isSafeRegex` misses alternation-based ReDoS patterns like `(a|a)+$`. Independently reproduced: 28-char input takes 2703ms. Requires repo write access to `.team/runbooks/` to exploit. Backlog item carried from prior reviews.
🔴 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:6` — `mkdirSync` imported but never used. Remove from import.
